steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank completes a "from...to" range: the temperature went from one value at 11:00 a.m. to 16.2°C at 7:00 p.m. The blank must supply "from 18.4°C at" — the question is purely about punctuation. The preposition "from" must connect directly to its object (18.4°C) with no intervening punctuation, and no comma or colon should disrupt the range structure.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — from, 18.4°C at: places a comma between "from" and its object "18.4°C." A comma cannot separate a preposition from its object — this breaks the grammatical bond. ✗
  • Choice B — from, 18.4°C at,: same error as A (comma after "from"), plus an additional comma after "at" that further fragments the range. Two punctuation errors. ✗
  • Choice C — from 18.4°C at: no punctuation between "from" and "18.4°C," and no punctuation after "at." The range reads smoothly: "from 18.4°C at 11:00 a.m. to 16.2°C at 7:00 p.m." ✓
  • Choice D — from: 18.4°C at,: uses a colon after "from," which is never correct — a colon cannot follow a preposition. Also adds a spurious comma after "at." ✗
That confirms C is correct.
